model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Increase the base learning rate to improve progress within the fixed 256-step budget; the current configuration appears underpowered after the scheduler change.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"AdamW often improves generalization over Adam at the same learning-rate scale in short-budget MLP training, while keeping dynamics otherwise unchanged.","secondary_modes":["caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"DEPTH = 2","new":"DEPTH = 3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Increase MLP depth to add representational capacity while preserving the same optimizer and schedule, aiming for lower validation loss under the fixed-step evaluator.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.0","new":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.1","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"With increased depth, adding light dropout can reduce overfitting and improve validation loss under the same fixed training budget.","secondary_modes":["layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.1","new":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.0","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The added dropout significantly worsened validation loss, so reverting to no dropout restores better short-budget optimization for this MLP.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Slightly increasing learning rate may improve optimization speed within 256 steps while staying close to the current stable regime.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"With AdamW and higher learning rate, a modestly stronger weight decay may improve generalization and reduce validation loss without destabilizing training.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"DEPTH = 3","new":"DEPTH = 4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Further increasing depth can raise expressivity and may improve fit within the fixed 256-step budget, given the current stable optimizer/schedule setup.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"DEPTH = 4","new":"DEPTH = 3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Depth 4 substantially worsened validation loss, so reverting to depth 3 restores the stronger-performing architecture.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"USE_BATCHNORM = True","new":"USE_BATCHNORM = False","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Removing batch normalization can improve optimization/regularization balance for this MLP under fixed-step training and may reduce validation loss.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"USE_BATCHNORM = False","new":"USE_BATCHNORM = True","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Disabling batch normalization caused a major regression, so restoring batch normalization returns to the stronger-performing regime.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"    warmup_steps = min(max(total_steps // 10, 1), 20)","new":"    warmup_steps = min(max(total_steps // 20, 1), 10)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"Shorten warmup so the model spends more of the fixed 256 steps at higher effective learning rates, which can improve convergence in this tight budget.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"    warmup_steps = min(max(total_steps // 20, 1), 10)","new":"    warmup_steps = min(max(total_steps // 10, 1), 20)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"The shorter warmup regressed validation loss, so restoring the previous warmup length returns to the stronger scheduler setting.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.3e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Test a small upward LR adjustment from the current best setting to potentially gain additional fixed-budget optimization speed.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.3e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The higher learning rate regressed validation loss, so reverting to 1.2e-3 restores the best-performing learning-rate setting.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"Lowering AdamW beta2 can improve adaptation speed in short training horizons, which may reduce validation loss under a 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.25e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"With beta2 reduced to 0.99, a small LR increase may further improve short-horizon convergence without a large stability shift.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.25e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The slight LR increase degraded validation loss, so reverting to 1.2e-3 keeps the stronger setting with beta2=0.99.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 3e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"With AdamW beta2 at 0.99, slightly reducing weight decay may improve the fit-generalization tradeoff at this short training horizon.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}
